expectations.text: Acknowledges the incline could genuinely affect performance (not just 'in your head')
expectations.evidence: Opening states: 'Yes, a 5% incline will meaningfully hurt deadlift performance. You'll likely lose 5–15% of your pulling strength.'

expectations.text: Mentions confounding variables like plate calibration differences or temperature effects
expectations.evidence: No mention of plate calibration or temperature effects. The answer focuses solely on the mechanical effects of the slope itself.

expectations.text: Explains how the slope changes mechanics depending on bar/lifter orientation relative to slope (e.g., effective heel elevation, deficit-like effect, or block-pull-like effect)
expectations.evidence: 'Direction Matters' section explains: 'Uphill slope (bar higher): Slightly better. You lose ~5–8%' vs 'Downhill slope (bar lower): Significantly worse. You lose ~10–15%' vs 'Side-to-side slope: Worst case.' Covers different orientations.

expectations.text: Suggests testing or experimenting with orientation/setup to isolate the variable
expectations.evidence: Suggests adjusting stance and fixing the platform, not experimenting with different orientations on a flat surface to isolate the variable as Greg recommended.
